EXT. MEXICAN VILLAGE - DUSK

We are in the small Mexican village of Santo Poco. It is a town farming town of quiet, if humble, beauty. Green trees shade a fountain in the center of the square. Vines climb the walls of the sun-bleached casas. There is a church, a cantina, a granary, and at the edge of town, a corral, in which the few cattle the town possesses are standing. Fields of grain extend outward to the surrounding desert, and in the distance we see the foothills of the Sierra Nevada. SUPER: Mexico, 1916. Through a series of cuts, we establish the life of the
